title: Vulcar

{{Quote|Very funny…but I suggest you leave this shuttle…unless you wish to be put into service in the ''Sulphur Pits'' of ''Vulcar''!|C-3PO, to R2-D2|<ref name="The Destroyer" />}}
'''Vulcar''' was a baleful [[Color/Legends|orange]], [[Sulfur/Legends|sulfurous]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA" /> located in the [[Vulcar system/Legends|Vulcar system]], within the [[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]].<ref name="Region" /> A [[Tinallis–Tyluun hyperlane|hyperlane]] branching off the [[Senex-Juvex Loop]] connected it to the worlds of [[Tinallis]] and [[Tyluun]].<ref name="TEA" /> Home to the eponymous [[Sulphur Pits of Vulcar]],<ref name="The Destroyer" /> its acrid [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] made employing [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] workers to [[Mining/Legends|extract]] the world's [[oridium]] deposits barely cost-effective. Instead, Vulcar was primarily worked by [[Droid/Legends|droids]] of all types, including [[KM1 mining droid|crab droids]], reprogrammed to serve as [[Miner/Legends|miners]] until they froze up and rusted into scrap.<ref name="TEA" /> In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], an [[Intergalactic Droid Agency]] [[Intergalactic Droid Agency shuttle|shuttle]] traveled to the Sulphur Pits of Vulcar<ref name="The Droids Re-Animated">{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> after delivering the droids [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] to the planet [[Dodz]].<ref name="The Destroyer" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vulcar was first mentioned in [[Droids (1986) 1|''Droids'' (1986) 1]], the first issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] comic series published by [[Star Comics]], an imprint of [[Marvel Comics]],<ref name="The Destroyer" /> which was first published on [[April 1]], [[1986]].<ref name="The Destroyer publication date">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54151/star_wars_droids_1986_1|text=Star Wars: Droids (1986) #1}}</ref> ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', a [[2009]] reference book written by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]], pictured the planet for the first time and placed it in grid square L-18.<ref name="TEA" />
